The plant Batrachium, also known as Ranunculaceae or water crowfoot, is a genus of aquatic plants characterized by their floating or submerged leaves and delicate white flowers. The health benefits of Batrachium include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Batrachium include ranunculin, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antitumor activity, as well as other alkaloids and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Batrachium include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Batrachium setosissimum is classified as a species of aquatic plant in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. It is further grouped into the genus Batrachium, which consists of several species of aquatic plants. The genus Batrachium is itself part of the larger order Ranunculales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Batrachium setosissimum from other related species.
